question_text: An electromagnet is connected to a 6V power supply with a current of 2A flowing through it. If you increase the voltage to 12V, what current now flows through the electromagnet (assuming the resistance stays the same)?
correct_answer: 4A. Using V = IR, if V doubles and R stays the same, then I must double. So 2A × 2 = 4A.
explanation: When you double the voltage and resistance stays the same, the current doubles according to Ohm's Law (V = IR). A stronger current makes the electromagnet stronger.
